title: Mellow Crisp Spanish Potatoes Tortilla Espanola
description: A winey, crisp, and comforting Spanish dish featuring parmesan and katsuramuki-cut potatoes, inspired by the cuisine of Andalusia. This tortilla espanola is a twist on the traditional recipe, with a focus on ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs and a pinch of salt.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the onions and garlic until softened.
Add the katsuramuki-cut potatoes to the skillet and cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the red wine to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring to deglaze the pan.
In a separate bowl, combine the grated parmesan cheese and a pinch of black pepper.
Add the cooked potato mixture to the bowl with the eggs and stir to combine.
Pour the egg mixture into a greased 9-inch round baking dish and top with the parmesan cheese mixture.
Bake the tortilla espanola in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the center is set.
Remove the tortilla espanola from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley, if desired.
